data.title: Former executive of blood filter start-up charged for concealing deaths. ExThera lured patients to Antigua; ex-chief regulatory officer could face three-year sentence.
data.description: ExThera lured cancer patients to Antigua by claiming its devices could provide a cure, resulting in legal issues. The company's ex-chief regulatory officer is now facing a possible sentence of up to three years in prison.

data.title: Mission Success: Rocket Lab Completes 83rd Launch
data.description: Rocket Lab Corporation, a renowned provider of launch services and space systems traded on Nasdaq under the ticker RKLB, achieved another milestone on March 5, 2026, in Mahia, New Zealand. The Company successfully completed its second launch within one week from two distinct countries. This accomplishment underscores Rocket Lab's dominance in small launch services, solidifying its reputation as a global leader in the space industry. This latest feat highlights the Company's rapid pace and efficiency, demonstrating its ability to execute successful launches in quick succession to meet the growing demand for small satellite deployment.

data.title: Former executive at blood filter start-up indicted for concealing deaths of patients in Antigua. Facing up to 3 years in jail.
data.description: Formerly employed at ExThera, the chief regulatory officer enticed cancer patients to Antigua by guaranteeing that the company's devices could provide a cure. This individual is now facing a potential prison sentence of up to three years as a consequence of their actions.
